events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario: shackle side loaded during rigging configuration, work in progress. Side loading a shackle is unsafe; shackles are designed for in-line loading. ASME B30 and OSHA 1926.251 require proper rigging. Side loading can reduce WLL significantly and cause failure. Decision: fail. Risk: high or critical. Component conditions: non-compliant, possibly installed-defective. Immediate controls: stop lift, reconfigure rigging to load shackle in line, use appropriate shackle or swivel, inspect shackle for damage.

    "Shackle is side loaded, which is outside its intended loading axis and significantly reduces working load limit",
    "Rigging configuration does not meet ASME B30/OSHA 1926.251 requirements for proper shackle loading"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop the lift immediately and lower/secure the load",
    "Reconfigure rigging so the shackle is loaded in-line through the pin and bow",
    "Use a swivel, spreader, or correct shackle type/orientation to eliminate side loading",
    "Inspect shackle and pin for deformation or damage before reuse",
    "Verify revised rigging against engineered lift plan before resuming"
  ],
  "rationale": "Side loading a shackle creates a high risk of shackle failure or pin deformation because the rated working load limit assumes in-line loading. ASME B30 and OSHA 1926.251 require rigging gear to be used within its rated capacity and intended loading configuration.",
